case_name: Professional Conduct Committee appointed by the Medical Council of New Zealand v H [2020] NZHPDT 1105 (17 August 2020)

summary.brief: Professional Conduct Committee appointed by the Medical Council of New Zealand v H [2020] NZHPDT 1105 (17 August 2020). Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: The submissions went through the individual particulars and concluded that separately or cumulatively the Practitioner’s conduct amounted to a departure from acceptable standards and warranted disciplinary sanction.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal finding

evidence_index.text: The Practitioner’s misconduct in this respect has been found by the Tribunal to be a refusal on his part to provide the treatment advice that was needed as noted above.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal finding

evidence_index.text: Once an adverse finding has been made, the probability must be that public interest considerations will require that the name of the practitioner be published in a preponderance of cases.
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal reasoning

evidence_index.text: On [ ] 2016, Dr H was rude and verbally abusive towards a colleague, Dr Y, during a phone call regarding the care of a patient, Ms A, who presented at [ ] Hospital when Dr H was the on-call [ ];
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal finding

evidence_index.text: On [ ] 2014, Dr H, while rostered as an on-call [ ], failed to come to the hospital within a reasonable timeframe when asked by Dr E to attend to see a patient, Ms L;
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal finding

evidence_index.text: On [ ] 2017, Dr H refused to provide treatment advice to Dr B during a phone call regarding the care of Ms T, who presented at [ ] Hospital when Dr H was the on-call [ ].
evidence_index.speaker: Tribunal finding

focused_exam.system: Examination mentioned but findings not specified

requestables.name: Phone call with Dr Y
requestables.description: Details of the phone call between Dr H and Dr Y regarding patient care

reveal.result_summary: Dr H was rude and verbally abusive
reveal.decision_impact: Contributed to a breakdown in professional communication

timeline_phases.name: Initial Contact
timeline_phases.description: Dr H was contacted by Dr Y regarding patient care

key_events.event: Phone call between Dr H and Dr Y

decision_points.clinical_context: Dr H was on-call and contacted by Dr Y regarding patient care
decision_points.prompt: How should Dr H have responded to the call from Dr Y?

options.description: Respond professionally and provide necessary treatment advice
options.clinical_reasoning: Maintains professional standards and supports team communication

options.description: Respond rudely and refuse to provide treatment advice

actual_action_defendant.description: Dr H responded rudely and refused to provide treatment advice

expected_action_court.description: Dr H should have responded professionally and provided treatment advice

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Dr H's response was unprofessional and disrupted team communication
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Dr H should have provided treatment advice and maintained professional conduct

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Professional response would have maintained team communication

factual_timeline.event: Phone call between Dr H and Dr Y
